codeReview.summary: The feature implementation covers the required behavior: exact deduplication by page+offset, overlap resolution by confidence, adjacent same-type merges, mode default maps for both redaction modes, and getDefaultDecision(mode+confidence) logic with threshold handling. Tests comprehensively exercise required merger and mode behaviors.

issues.description: Adjacent-entity text merge drops separator characters by concatenating `first.text + second.text` directly. When merged spans are separated by whitespace (or small allowed gaps), the merged text becomes collapsed (e.g., "JohnMartinez"), which conflicts with the merger rule example expecting "John Martinez" and can degrade displayed entity text.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture docs contain stale entity-type examples that do not match the current EntityType union, which can mislead implementation/tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:111-114 lists 'SSN', 'DRIVERS_LICENSE', and 'FULL_NAME'; current source of truth uses 'US_SSN', 'PERSON', and no 'DRIVERS_LICENSE' (src/core/detectors/entities.ts). This confusion appears in tests/unit/modes.test.ts:78 comment.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Merger tie-break conventions for multi-layer overlap are documented in _docs but not surfaced in AGENTS/architecture skill-critical references, increasing risk of drift as NER layer work is added.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: _docs/02-detection-layer.md:532-544 specifies "Layer 1 wins ties" and same-span conflict handling, but src/core/detectors/merger.ts:109-112 currently only compares confidence for exact-span dedup.

summary: Reviewed transcript skeleton, handoff, skill guidance, and commit 90087d6 for merger-and-modes. Feature meets required milestone behavior and is marked pass, with one non-blocking text-merge correctness issue and two shared-state documentation/convention gaps noted.
